title: Admin double-auth (ID/password + Internet Identity), remove Quick Access cards, clean homepage

requirements.summary: Remove Quick Access cards (User Panel / Admin Panel) from the homepage, keeping only hero section, tournaments, and other public content
acceptanceCriteria: No 'User Panel' or 'Admin Panel' quick access cards are visible on the homepage
Hero section, featured tournaments, and CTA buttons remain intact
No broken layout after card removal

file_operations.description: Remove the Quick Access cards section that displays User Panel and Admin Panel navigation shortcuts. Keep the hero section with CTA buttons and any existing tournament listings or features grid intact. Ensure the remaining layout flows properly without gaps or broken spacing.

requirements.summary: Ensure the main homepage (/) is user-facing with tournament browsing and register/login access, with no admin-facing elements
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage shows tournament listings or a CTA to browse tournaments
Register and Login links/buttons are accessible from the homepage
No admin controls or admin shortcuts are present on the homepage

file_operations.description: Verify that the homepage displays public tournament information or a clear CTA to browse tournaments. Ensure Register and Login buttons are accessible via the Header component. Remove any admin-specific UI elements or shortcuts if present. This operation validates the public-facing nature of the homepage after REQ-1 card removal.

requirements.summary: Implement two-step admin authentication flow for /admin: Step 1 validates hardcoded credentials (Username: 'Empire Esports', Password: 'Shivam803119&'); Step 2 requires Internet Identity login with principal registration and verification
acceptanceCriteria: Visiting /admin shows a credential form (username + password) before anything else
Wrong credentials block access and show an error message
After correct credentials, user is prompted to login with Internet Identity
The first Internet Identity principal to complete admin login is saved as the sole admin principal in backend stable storage
Subsequent admin logins require the same Internet Identity principal; any other principal is rejected with an error
A successfully authenticated admin can access the full admin dashboard
Admin session state is persisted in localStorage so the user stays logged in across page refreshes
Admin can logout, which clears both credential and Internet Identity session state
Multiple devices can access admin panel using the same Internet Identity principal

file_operations.description: Replace the current single-step admin authentication with a two-step flow. Step 1: Validate hardcoded credentials (Username: 'Empire Esports', Password: 'Shivam803119&'). Step 2: Integrate with useInternetIdentity hook to trigger Internet Identity login after credential verification. Add state flags: credentialsVerified (boolean), identityVerified (boolean), and isAdminAuthenticated (both must be true). After II login, call backend methods registerAdminPrincipal or isAdminPrincipal to register or verify the principal. Persist both credentialsVerified and identityVerified flags in localStorage. On logout, clear both flags, clear Internet Identity session via the clear() function from useInternetIdentity, and invalidate React Query cache. Expose loginWithCredentials(username, password), loginWithIdentity(), logout(), and the state flags. Handle errors from backend principal verification and surface them to the UI.

Refactor the admin login page to render a two-step UI. Step 1: Display a username and password form with fields for 'Empire Esports' and 'Shivam803119&'. On form submit, call AdminAuthContext.loginWithCredentials(). Show an error message if credentials are incorrect. On success, transition to Step 2. Step 2: Display an 'Authenticate with Internet Identity' button. On click, call AdminAuthContext.loginWithIdentity(). Show a loading spinner during II authentication. Display error messages if the Internet Identity principal does not match the registered admin principal (e.g., 'Unauthorized: This Internet Identity is not registered as admin'). On full success (both steps complete), redirect to /admin dashboard. Use conditional rendering to show only the current step based on AdminAuthContext.credentialsVerified state. Ensure clear visual feedback for each step and any errors.

requirements.summary: Update AdminAuthContext to implement the two-step flow with credential validation and Internet Identity principal registration/verification
acceptanceCriteria: AdminAuthContext exposes credentialsVerified and isAdminAuthenticated flags
isAdminAuthenticated is true only when both credential and Internet Identity steps succeed
Logout clears all admin session state from localStorage
Context uses useInternetIdentity hook for II integration

file_operations.description: This requirement is covered by REQ-3 frontend file operations. Ensure the context exposes credentialsVerified, identityVerified, and isAdminAuthenticated (computed as credentialsVerified && identityVerified). Use useInternetIdentity to access identity.getPrincipal() after II login. Call actor.registerAdminPrincipal(principal) on first login or actor.isAdminPrincipal(principal) on subsequent logins. Store flags in localStorage keys 'adminCredentialsVerified' and 'adminIdentityVerified'. On logout, remove both keys, call clear() from useInternetIdentity, and call queryClient.clear() to reset all cached data.

requirements.summary: Update AdminLoginPage to render a two-step UI with credential form and Internet Identity authentication button
acceptanceCriteria: Step 1 shows username and password fields with a submit button
Wrong credentials show an error and do not advance to Step 2
Step 2 shows an 'Login with Internet Identity' button
If the Internet Identity principal is rejected, an error message is shown
On full success, user is redirected to /admin dashboard
Loading spinner shown during Internet Identity authentication

file_operations.description: This requirement is covered by REQ-3 frontend file operations. Render the credential form in Step 1 with username and password inputs. Display inline error messages for wrong credentials. Only show Step 2 (Internet Identity button) after credentialsVerified is true. Show a loading spinner using loginStatus from useInternetIdentity or a local loading state during II authentication. Display backend errors (e.g., principal mismatch) in an error alert. Use useNavigate to redirect to /admin on successful authentication (isAdminAuthenticated === true). Ensure UI is responsive and accessible with proper labels and ARIA attributes.

requirements.summary: Remove the admin shield/link from the Header component so regular users cannot navigate to the admin panel from the main site header
acceptanceCriteria: No admin icon, link, or shortcut appears in the public site header
Admin panel is only accessible by directly typing /admin in the URL bar

file_operations.description: Remove the admin shield icon and /admin navigation link from the Header component. This includes removing the shield icon from both desktop and mobile navigation menus. Ensure the remaining navigation items (Home, Tournaments, Terms & Conditions, Login/Register/Logout) remain functional and properly styled. The admin panel will only be accessible by directly typing /admin in the browser's address bar.
